mission.title: open-cluster-management: Natively support OCM on EKS
mission.description: Natively support OCM on EKS. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current OCM installation on EKS hub
steps.description:
Verify your OCM hub cluster installation on EKS:
```bash
clusteradm get hub-info
kubectl get pods -n open-cluster-management
kubectl get managedclusters
```
This feature addresses the limitation that OCM originally only supported x509 certificate-based authentication, which is not natively supported by EKS.

steps.title: Initialize the OCM hub on EKS with token-based authentication
steps.description:
EKS does not support the default x509 certificate authentication used by OCM. Use token-based authentication or AWS IAM-based registration instead:
```bash
# Initialize the hub cluster on EKS
clusteradm init --use-bootstrap-token

# Verify the hub is running
kubectl get pods -n open-cluster-management
clusteradm get token
```
The `--use-bootstrap-token` flag enables token-based registration which is compatible with EKS.

steps.title: Join managed clusters to the EKS hub
steps.description:
Register managed (spoke) clusters with the EKS hub using the bootstrap token:
```bash
# On the hub, get the join command
clusteradm get token | grep clusteradm

# On each managed cluster, run the join command
clusteradm join --hub-token <token> --hub-apiserver <eks-api-url> --cluster-name <cluster-name>

# On the hub, accept the managed cluster
clusteradm accept --clusters <cluster-name>
```
For cross-account AWS scenarios, the hub creates IAM roles for each spoke cluster. Non-EKS spokes can join by providing AWS credentials to the registration agent.

steps.title: Verify managed clusters are registered
steps.description:
Confirm that managed clusters have joined the EKS hub successfully:
```bash
kubectl get managedclusters
kubectl get managedclustersets
clusteradm get hub-info
```
All managed clusters should show `ManagedClusterConditionAvailable=True` status.

resolution.summary: OCM added native EKS support by introducing token-based and AWS IAM-based authentication for cluster registration, removing the x509 certificate requirement. Hub clusters on EKS use bootstrap tokens for managed cluster registration, with cross-account IAM role support for multi-account AWS deployments.
codeSnippets:
# Initialize OCM hub on EKS with token-based auth
clusteradm init --use-bootstrap-token

# Join a managed cluster
clusteradm join --hub-token <token> --hub-apiserver <eks-api-url> --cluster-name my-cluster

prerequisites.description: An EKS cluster for the hub and one or more Kubernetes clusters (EKS or other) to register as managed clusters. Requires the clusteradm CLI.
